title: QaLedgerItem
description: Structured exploratory-QA ledger item (`f3-qa-explore-core`, Feature #3710, Epic #3686). One object per observation captured while exploring a consumer surface — distinct from a `qa-finding` (the browser-sweep `F#` finding): a ledger item classifies a broader exploratory signal (product bug, environment-setup friction, tooling/DX gap, test-coverage gap, or enhancement idea), records the evidence and coverage context, names any missing test, and carries the operator disposition so Triage can parse a whole session. A ledger item has a two-phase lifecycle: the read-only **Capture** phase appends an item with the Capture-phase fields populated (`id`, `class`, `severity`, `evidence`, `coverage`, `missingTest`) but no resolved `disposition`; the **Triage** phase later assigns the `disposition`. The schema therefore validates BOTH the captured-but-untriaged shape (`disposition` absent, `null`, or a `pending`/`untriaged` sentinel — the rolling backlog `.agents/scripts/lib/qa/qa-session.js` reads back on resume) AND the fully-triaged shape (`disposition` resolved to `file`/`defer`/`dismiss`), while still rejecting genuinely malformed items. Evidence MUST already be scrubbed of secrets, session tokens, and PII per `.agents/rules/security-baseline.md` before it is rendered here.

id.description: Stable ledger-item identifier, assigned `L1`, `L2`, … in capture order across an exploratory session.

class.description: The kind of exploratory signal this item represents. `product-bug` is a genuine defect in the product under test; `environment-setup` is friction standing up or seeding the environment; `tooling-dx` is a developer-experience or tooling gap; `test-gap` is a missing or insufficient automated test; `enhancement` is a non-defect improvement idea.

severity.description: Operator-assigned severity of the item, used to order Triage attention.

evidence.description: A one-line, scrubbed description of the observed signal supporting the item (what was seen, where). Captured detail MUST be free of tokens, session cookies, and PII per `.agents/rules/security-baseline.md`.

coverage.description: The surface, scenario, or area of the product the exploration was covering when this item surfaced (e.g. a route label or feature name). Falls back to `unknown` when the explorer cannot resolve a coverage label.

missingTest.description: A description of the automated test that, if present, would have caught this signal — or `null` when no test gap applies (e.g. a pure enhancement idea).

disposition.description: Triage output. Once an item is triaged, this resolves to what Triage should do with it: `file` to promote it to a follow-up ticket, `defer` to park it for a later session, or `dismiss` to close it as non-actionable. While the item is still in the read-only Capture phase (captured-but-untriaged), `disposition` is `null`, absent, or carries a `pending`/`untriaged` sentinel — the rolling-backlog state `.agents/scripts/lib/qa/qa-session.js` reads back on resume. Any non-sentinel string value MUST be one of the triaged enum values; an out-of-enum value (e.g. `maybe`) is still rejected.

relates.description: Optional. The ids of other ledger items this item relates to or folds into during session triage. Omit (or leave empty) for a standalone item.

routedTo.description: Optional finding-to-issue link recording where Triage routed this item. Populated only after the item is promoted to a follow-up ticket (`disposition: file`); omit while the item is still in Capture or was deferred/dismissed. The same link shape is mirrored on `qa-finding.schema.json`.

issue.description: The GitHub issue number the item was routed to.

url.description: The canonical URL of the routed issue.

kind.description: The kind of ticket the item was routed to: a `story`, an `epic`, or a bare `issue`.
